comment: v2 — pruned from the original. Removed the 4 redundant customer-lookup tools, the 5 noise tools (helper, process, validate, tool_3_v2, get_data), and collapsed customer lookups into one canonical lookup_customer. fetch_customer_v2_databricks renamed to get_customer_metrics with USE-WHEN trigger language.

tools.name: get_ticket
tools.description: ALWAYS call this first to pull the full ticket record (submitter, subject, body, priority, created_at).

tools.name: lookup_customer
tools.description: Look up a customer by email or customer_id. Call once per ticket, right after get_ticket, to resolve the submitter. Returns customer_id, name, tier, tier_since, sso_provider, primary_contact, billing_contact, seats_used, seats_limit, mrr. This is the only customer lookup you need — do not call any other lookup tool.

tools.name: check_plan_entitlements
tools.description: USE WHEN: ticket asks about plan features, seat caps, included usage, rate limits as defined by plan. Returns the customer's tier-level entitlements.

tools.name: check_system_status
tools.description: USE WHEN: ticket mentions a possible outage, says "is your platform down", or you suspect multiple customers are affected. Returns subsystem health.

tools.name: get_customer_metrics
tools.description: USE WHEN: a customer reports rate limiting, usage anomalies, intermittent errors, slow performance, retry storms, unexplained 429s/500s, or any complaint that requires looking at their actual traffic. Returns request volume, per-endpoint error rate, retry counts, and current-vs-plan-limit headroom over the last N days (default 7). DO NOT answer rate-limit questions from plan config alone — call this first.

tools.name: search_prior_tickets
tools.description: USE WHEN: the ticket is a follow-up to a previous case, or the customer references a past incident.

tools.name: spawn_specialist
tools.description: Dispatch a specialist subagent for ONE category. If a ticket touches multiple categories, call this once per category — multiple specialists work in parallel.

tools.name: write_response
tools.description: Compose the final customer-facing response. Use only after every spawned specialist has returned. The response must address every issue raised in the ticket.

tools.name: escalate_to_human
tools.description: USE WHEN: any specialist reported it could not complete the work; OR the ticket involves legal/security/code-change intervention; OR you don't have a tool that fits and the answer requires human judgement.
